edges.description: Repeated avian antigen exposure triggers measurable bird-specific antibody responses.
Antigen exposure initiates the hypersensitivity-pneumonitis immune response that shifts across disease stages.
Immune-mediated interstitial lung disease from repeated avian antigen exposure clinically manifests with dyspnea.
Immune-mediated interstitial lung disease from repeated avian antigen exposure clinically manifests with cough.
Monocyte, macrophage, and dendritic-cell remodeling is modeled upstream of the stage-dependent T-cell cytokine shift.
edges.source: Expansion of Resident Monocytes and Interstitial Macrophages

edges.description: Acute inflammatory hypersensitivity pneumonitis can produce flu-like systemic symptoms.
Chronic inflammatory hypersensitivity pneumonitis can produce fatigue.
Lung inflammation and evolving interstitial injury produce exertional dyspnea.
Lung inflammation from hypersensitivity pneumonitis produces cough.
Active inflammatory interstitial lung disease can impair gas exchange.
Bird-antigen sensitization is part of the immune-mediated interstitial lung disease that manifests with dyspnea.
Bird-antigen sensitization is part of the immune-mediated interstitial lung disease that manifests with cough.
Enriched classical monocyte states feed the profibrotic macrophage differentiation trajectory.
SPP1-high lung macrophages are associated with the fibrotic remodeling phenotype in fibrotic hypersensitivity pneumonitis.
Cytotoxic T cells with TGF-beta, TNF-alpha, and NF-kappaB programs are modeled as part of the profibrotic inflammatory branch.
edges.source: Cytotoxic T Cell Program with Profibrotic Signaling

edges.description: MMP14-high macrophage promotion of fibroblast-to-myofibroblast transition contributes to pulmonary fibrosis.
TLR2 and NF-kappaB signaling regulate the MMP14-high macrophage profibrotic program.
edges.source: TLR2 and NF-κB Regulation of MMP14 and Exosome Secretion

nodes.description: Bird antigen exposure triggers B cell activation and elevated serum IgG/IgA to avian proteins in bird-related hypersensitivity pneumonitis.

evidence.reference_title: Screening and diagnosis of acute and chronic bird-related hypersensitivity pneumonitis by serum IgG and IgA antibodies to bird antigens with ImmunoCAP®.
evidence.snippet: The levels of sIgG/sIgA against the bird antigens of the three species were significantly higher in subjects with acute bird-related HP and chronic bird-related HP with acute episodes (recurrent type) than in the control subjects.

nodes.description: Fibrotic hypersensitivity pneumonitis shows elevated classical monocytes enriched for CCL3hi/CCL4hi and S100Ahi states.

evidence.reference_title: Single-Cell Analysis Reveals Novel Immune Perturbations in Fibrotic Hypersensitivity Pneumonitis.
evidence.snippet: Compared with control samples, FHP has elevated classical monocytes (adjusted-P = 2.5 × 10-3) and is enriched in CCL3hi/CCL4hi and S100Ahi classical monocytes (adjusted-P < 2.2 × 10-16).

nodes.description: Persistent or recurrent cough associated with hypersensitivity pneumonitis.

evidence.reference_title: [A case of acute bird fancier's lung caused by feather duvet].
evidence.snippet: A 57-year-old woman was admitted to our hospital because of cough and low-grade fever for 2 months and shortness of breath for 2 weeks.

nodes.description: Fibrotic hypersensitivity pneumonitis features GZMhi cytotoxic T cells with transcriptional programs implicating TGFβ, TNFα, and NFκB pathways.
nodes.id: Cytotoxic T Cell Program with Profibrotic Signaling

evidence.reference_title: Single-Cell Analysis Reveals Novel Immune Perturbations in Fibrotic Hypersensitivity Pneumonitis.
evidence.snippet: Compared with both control subjects and IPF, cells from patients with FHP are significantly enriched in GZMhi cytotoxic T cells. These cells exhibit TF activities indicative of TGFβ and TNFα and NFκB pathways.

nodes.description: Exertional or progressive shortness of breath associated with inflammatory and fibrotic lung involvement.

evidence.reference_title: [A case of acute bird fancier's lung caused by feather duvet].
evidence.snippet: A 57-year-old woman was admitted to our hospital because of cough and low-grade fever for 2 months and shortness of breath for 2 weeks.

evidence.reference_title: Advanced Imaging and Occupational History in the Diagnosis of Bird Fancier's Lung: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: We present the case of a 43-year-old male pigeon keeper with an eight-week history of progressive dyspnea on exertion and intermittent chest pain.

nodes.description: Bird antigen exposure is associated with increased resident monocytes, interstitial macrophages, and type 2 dendritic cells in the lung.
nodes.id: Expansion of Resident Monocytes and Interstitial Macrophages

evidence.snippet: Both groups presented increases in resident monocytes, interstitial macrophages and type 2 dendritic cells (DCs), but also reductions in inflammatory monocytes, alveolar macrophages and tolerogenic DCs compared with their control groups.

nodes.description: Repeated inhalation of avian proteins from bird droppings, feathers, or serum triggers bird-related hypersensitivity pneumonitis.

evidence.snippet: Bird-related hypersensitivity pneumonitis (BRHP) is an interstitial lung disease induced by avian proteins.

evidence.reference_title: Advanced Imaging and Occupational History in the Diagnosis of Bird Fancier's Lung: A Case Report.
evidence.snippet: Bird fancier's lung (BFL) is a subtype of hypersensitivity pneumonitis (HP), an immune-mediated interstitial lung disease (ILD) resulting from the repeated inhalation of avian proteins found in bird droppings, feathers, and serum.

evidence.reference_title: Bird Fancier's lung: An underdiagnosed etiology of dyspnea.
evidence.snippet: Bird Fancier's Lung is a type of hypersensitivity pneumonitis, an immunologically mediated lung disease due to repetitive exposure of air-borne avian antigen.

Chronically, these diseases may present with the gradual onset of cough, dyspnea on exertion, fatigue, anorexia, and weight loss which may progress to pulmonary fibrosis or severe pulmonary insufficiency.

nodes.description: Flu-like systemic symptoms during acute exposure episodes.

evidence.reference_title: [A case of acute bird fancier's lung caused by feather duvet].
evidence.snippet: A 57-year-old woman was admitted to our hospital because of cough and low-grade fever for 2 months and shortness of breath for 2 weeks.

nodes.description: Reduced blood oxygenation during active disease.

evidence.snippet: A 47-year-old woman, without significant past medical history, presented an acute dyspnea with hypoxia, marked pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) and signs of right heart failure.

nodes.description: MMP14-high macrophages with an M2-like phenotype promote fibroblast-to-myofibroblast transition in hypersensitivity pneumonitis models, contributing to fibrotic remodeling.

evidence.reference_title: MMP14(high) macrophages orchestrate progressive pulmonary fibrosis in SR-Ag-induced hypersensitivity pneumonitis.
evidence.snippet: we identified MMP14high macrophage subcluster with a predominant M2 phenotype that exhibited higher activity in promoting fibroblast-to myofibroblast transition (FMT).

nodes.description: Specific classical monocyte states differentiate into SPP1hi lung macrophages associated with fibrosis.

evidence.reference_title: Single-Cell Analysis Reveals Novel Immune Perturbations in Fibrotic Hypersensitivity Pneumonitis.
evidence.snippet: Trajectory analyses demonstrate that S100Ahi classical monocytes differentiate into SPP1hi lung macrophages associated with fibrosis.

nodes.description: Fibrotic remodeling in chronic or progressive disease.

evidence.reference_title: Pirfenidone in fibrotic hypersensitivity pneumonitis: a double-blind, randomised clinical trial of efficacy and safety.
evidence.snippet: Fibrotic hypersensitivity pneumonitis (FHP) is an irreversible lung disease with high morbidity and mortality.

evidence.reference_title: Nintedanib in patients with progressive fibrosing interstitial lung diseases-subgroup analyses by interstitial lung disease diagnosis in the INBUILD trial: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, parallel-group trial.
evidence.snippet: the rate of decline in FVC (mL/year) over 52 weeks in patients who received at least one dose of nintedanib or placebo in five prespecified subgroups based on the ILD diagnoses documented by the investigators: hypersensitivity pneumonitis

evidence.reference_title: Chronic hypersensitivity pneumonitis and pulmonary sarcoidosis: differentiation from usual interstitial pneumonia using high-resolution computed tomography.
evidence.snippet: In the 3 diseases, most important prognosis-predicting factor is the extent of fibrotic score (the extent of honeycombing and reticulation) calculated on high-resolution computed tomography scans or fibrosis estimated on chest radiographs.

evidence.reference_title: MMP14(high) macrophages orchestrate progressive pulmonary fibrosis in SR-Ag-induced hypersensitivity pneumonitis.
evidence.snippet: Importantly, it was observed that the transfer of MMP14-overexpressing macrophages into mice promoted lung inflammation and fibrosis induced by SR-Ag.

nodes.description: TLR2 and NF-κB signaling regulate MMP14 expression and macrophage exosome secretion in antigen-stimulated hypersensitivity pneumonitis models.
nodes.id: TLR2 and NF-κB Regulation of MMP14 and Exosome Secretion

evidence.reference_title: MMP14(high) macrophages orchestrate progressive pulmonary fibrosis in SR-Ag-induced hypersensitivity pneumonitis.
evidence.snippet: We demonstrated that suppressing toll-like receptor 2 (TLR2) and nuclear factor kappa-B (NF-κB) could attenuate MMP14 expression and exosome secretion in macrophages stimulation with SR-Ag.

nodes.description: Early disease shows a mixed Th1/Th2 response, while progression shifts toward a Th2/Th17 mixed response.

evidence.snippet: In the first stages of BRHP, there is a mixed Th1/Th2 immune response, while during the progression of the disease, although there is a Th1 response, the cytokine levels seem to indicate a switch towards a Th2/Th17 mixed response.
